features.summary: Apple reported record Q1 FY2025 revenue of $124.3 billion, up 4% year-over-year, with record EPS of $2.40 (up 10%). iPhone revenue was $69.1 billion (flat YoY), Mac grew 16% to $9 billion, iPad increased 15% to $8.1 billion, and Services reached a record $26.3 billion (up 14%). The company achieved record installed base of 2.35 billion active devices and record upgrader levels for iPhone. Apple Intelligence launched in October with additional features and countries added in December, showing stronger iPhone 16 performance in markets where it's available.
features.management_outlook: Management expects March quarter total revenue to grow low-to-mid single digits year-over-year despite a 2.5 percentage point foreign exchange headwind. Services revenue is expected to grow low-double digits. Apple Intelligence will expand to additional languages including French, German, Italian, Portuguese, Spanish, Japanese, Korean, and simplified Chinese in April. The company remains optimistic about its product pipeline and sees significant opportunities in emerging markets, particularly India where they're opening four new stores.
features.risks: China revenue declined 11% year-over-year with over half the decline attributed to channel inventory reduction, highlighting competitive pressures in the world's most competitive smartphone market. Foreign exchange headwinds are expected to negatively impact revenue by 2.5 percentage points in the March quarter. Apple Intelligence is not yet available in China, potentially impacting iPhone demand in that key market. Regulatory challenges continue to affect the services business globally.
features.key_drivers: Apple Intelligence adoption is driving stronger iPhone 16 performance in available markets, with the iPhone 16 family outperforming iPhone 15 from launch through December quarter. Record upgrader levels and growing installed base provide foundation for future growth. Strong momentum in emerging markets with record revenues in Latin America, Middle East, and South Asia. Services business benefits from over 1 billion paid subscriptions and increasing customer engagement across all offerings.
